title: Vice President - Loans Operations - Operations
description:
Company overview
Nomura is a global financial services group with an integrated network spanning approximately 30 countries and regions. By connecting markets East & West, Nomura services the needs of individuals, institutions, corporates and governments through its three business divisions: Wealth Management, Investment Management, and Wholesale (Global Markets and Investment Banking). Founded in 1925, the firm is built on a tradition of disciplined entrepreneurship, serving clients with creative solutions and considered thought leadership. For further information about Nomura, visit www.nomura.com

Department Overview
Nomura Loan Operations supports the Asia Ex-Japan Securitized Products & Private Credit Businesses. This is a high growth business, driven by innovative product portfolio, highly experienced team and strong franchise position, that operates in a rapidly expanding market segment.
Loans Operations works closely with the Loans Origination and Trading desk providing support in all pre and post trade execution, product life cycle to ensure accurate risk capture in the firm’s books and records.  The candidate will act as the face-off between the Trading Desk and other stakeholders, identify and remediate control risks, maintain strong governance and drive strategic change initiatives.

Responsibilities

  • Portfolio Operations & Life Cycle Management

o   Oversee end-to-end life cycle events and controls to ensure loans transactions are processed timely and accurately 
o    Monitor portfolio activity, pipeline and volume to assess operational capacity requirements
o    Validate deal documentation and ensure correct system/product set-up to capture risks correctly. 
o    Provide trade support for other products traded by the Loans desk including vanilla cash Bonds and Equities, vanilla derivative products, and credit insurance

  • Control, Risk Management and Governance

o    Maintain strong operational controls to ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements and audit standards
o    Lead and perform root cause analysis and implement action plans to improve controls/ productivity o    Review and present risk and control updates to senior management. Ensure risks are captured with appropriate action plans to address and manage risks indicators within risk threshold
o    Strong understanding of front to back trade flows as well as Risk and P&L
o    Assess new product initiatives and ensure operational readiness to support with appropriate controls 

  • Stakeholder Management 

o    Main Operational contact for Front Office and regional stakeholders across functions (legal, compliance, Finance, other operational teams, etc).
o    Drive engagement with stakeholders to plan and deliver new deal origination, new product launches and complex transaction planning. o    Handle escalations, resolve issues within agreed timeline and ensure smooth coordination across functions to support the business 
o    Liaise with external parties including trade counterparties, agents, banks, intermediaries, to ensure timely trade execution and settlement

  • Process Improvements 

o    Drive automation and digitalization initiatives to improve productivity and enhance controls
o    Implement best practices and utilize data to drive reviews and facilitate decision making. 

Requirements

  • Minimum 10 years experience supporting loan products.
  • Demonstrable experience reviewing, interpreting trade documentation aligned to LMA/APLMA standards for Private Credit Business.
  • Hands-on experience in syndicated loans, bilateral lending, agency servicing and loan products
  • Prior experience with Loan IQ
  • General knowledge of other Global Market products
  • The candidate will need to manage and develop the team to deliver multiple priorities and support the business
  • Strong stakeholder communication with confidence to challenge and escalate appropriately
  • High attention to detail, with strong numerical skills and ability to manage operational risks
  • Proven capability to manage complex transactions / resolve exceptions
  • Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.  Demonstrating a strong sense of ownership and commitment to delivery
  • Possesses strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and ability to adapt to dynamic situations
  • Self-motivated person, not afraid to suggest changes and re-invest process 

Nomura Competencies
Explore Insights & Vision

  • Identify the underlying causes of problems faced by you or your team and define a clear vision and direction for the future.

Making Strategic Decisions

  • Evaluate all the options for resolving the problems and effectively prioritize actions or recommendations.

Inspire Entrepreneurship in People

  • Inspire team members through effective communication of ideas and motivate them to actively enhance productivity.

Elevate Organizational Capability

  • Engage proactively in professional development and enhance team productivity through the promotion of knowledge sharing. 

Inclusion 

  • Respect DEI, foster a culture of psychological safety in the workplace and cultivate a "Risk Culture" (Challenge, Escalate and Respect).

Diversity Statement
Nomura is committed to an employment policy of equal opportunities, and is fundamentally opposed to any less favourable treatment accorded to existing or potential members of staff on the grounds of race, creed, colour, nationality, disability, marital status, pregnancy, gender or sexual orientation.

DISCLAIMER:  This Job Description is for reference only, and whilst this is intended to be an accurate reflection of the current job, it is not necessarily an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, skills, efforts, requirements or working conditions associated with the job.  The management reserves the right to revise the job and may, at his or her discretion, assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this job at any time.  
Nomura is an Equal Opportunity Employer

postedCompany.description: Nomura is a leading financial services group and the preeminent Asian-based investment bank with worldwide reach. Nomura provides a broad range of innovative solutions tailored to the specific requirements of individual, institutional, corporate and government clients through an international network in over 30 countries. Based in Tokyo and with regional headquarters in Hong Kong, London, and New York, Nomura employs over 27,000 staff worldwide. Nomura’s unique understanding of Asia enables the company to make a difference for clients through three business divisions: retail, asset management, and wholesale (global markets and investment banking).
postedCompany.name: NOMURA SINGAPORE LIMITED

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
